titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Samuel Wells Williams Family Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by John Espy, Carol King, and staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday October 27, 2020 at 07:02

did.abstract: The papers include correspondence (comprising over half of the collection), manuscripts of Samuel Wells Williams's Syllabic Dictionary of the Chinese Language , themes and lecture notes by Frederick Wells Williams, diaries, newspaper clippings, articles on China, maps, and pictures. The bulk of the correspondence relates to S. W. Williams, missionary, diplomat, and sinologue. The period between 1845 and 1855 has extensive correspondence with missionaries and with James Dwight Dana and Matthew C. Perry, whom Williams accompanied on his mission to open Japan and on his return visit in 1854. Williams's letters to friends and family comment on progress made and their reception in Japan. In 1856 Williams became secretary and interpreter to the American Legation in China and many of the letters refer to Chinese problems of the following 20 years. His correspondents include, in addition to Dana and Perry, Anson Burlingame, Hamilton Fish, Asa Gray, Frederick Low, William Bradford Reed, and William Henry Seward. The remaining correspondence covers the period 1885 to 1939, encompassing the correspondence of F.W. Williams, Yale professor, and Wayland Wells Williams, writer.

accessrestrict.head: Information about Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.
Series I. Correspondence, 1824-1939, is available on microfilm. Patrons must use FILM HM 277 instead of the originals.
Series II. Samuel Wells Williams, Journal: trip to Japan with Commadore Perry, is available in digital form. Patrons must use digital use copies instead of the originals.

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: Samuel Wells Williams Family Papers (MS 547). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition note
acqinfo.p: Gift of Yale-in-China from the library of Frederick Wells Williams and of Mrs. Dalton V. Garstin, 1945-1946; and Cynthia G. Blackwell, 1980 (Accession 1981-M-002). Transfer from East Asian Curator, 2004 (Accession 2005-M-014). Gift of Huntington Williams III, 2009 (Accession 2010-M-027), 2012 (Accession 2013-M-006).

altformavail.head: Alternative Formats
altformavail.p: Correspondence and related papers, 1853-1854, of Samuel Wells Williams regarding his services as an interpreter of Commodore Matthew Perry's expedition to Japan is available on microfilm (96 frames on 1 reel, 35 mm.) from Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library, at cost. Order no. HM22.
Series I. Correspondence, 1824-1939 is available on microfilm (10 reels, 35 mm.) from Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library, at cost. Order no. HM277.
Series II. Samuel Wells Williams, Journal: trip to Japan with Commadore Perry (Box 29A, Folders 1 and 2), is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in five series and two additions; all accessions received prior to August 2009 have been incorporated into this arrangement: I. Correspondence, 1824-1939. II. Samuel Wells Williams papers, 1828-1905. III. Frederick Wells Williams papers, 1876-1927. IV. Papers of others, 1831-1941. V. Oversize folios, 1826-1886.

scopecontent.head: Description of the Papers
scopecontent.p: The collection covers three generations of the Williams Family: Samuel Wells Williams, 1812-1884, and his brother Robert Stanton Williams; Frederick Wells Williams, 1857-1928; and Wayland Wells Williams, 1888-1945. The correspondence covers the years 1824-1939, nearly two-thirds of which (10 boxes) relates to Samuel Wells Williams and his life in China and the U. S. In addition to its being the largest part of the collection, the Samuel Wells Williams's correspondence, is by far the richest part.

Throughout this collection there is a great deal of family correspondence. However, Samuel Wells Williams also corresponded with James Dwight Dana, Asa Gray, Anson Burlingame, Commodore Matthew C. Perry, Hamilton Fish, Frederick Low, William Bradford Reed, and William Henry Seward. The collection contains few Frederick Wells Williams's letters and none from Wayland Wells Williams. In the later period there is one Pearl Buck letter and a few William Henry Brewer letters.

Samuel Wells Williams, missionary, diplomat, and sinologue sailed for China in 1833 and for nearly forty years this was his home. He first began his work in Macao with the missionary press and repository. He aided others in translating Chinese texts and himself compiled one of the first comprehensive Chinese-English dictionaries, A Syllabic Dictionary of the Chinese Language .

The very early years of the correspondence - 1824 to 1844 - represent a small part of the collection. However there are several letters to and from James Dwight Dana and Peter Parker. The next period which is quite extensive is 1845 to 1855. This part includes correspondence with Elijah Coleman Bridgman, James Dwight Dana and other members of the missionary family in China. An important part of the correspondence falls within the years 1853 and 1854, for it was in this period that Matthew C. Perry and Samuel Wells Williams met. Numerous letters pass between the two. Samuel Wells Williams accompanied Perry on his mission to open Japan and on his return visit in 1854. This trip resulted in some exceedingly interesting letters to friends and family on the progress made and the reception received. In addition, there are copies in Chinese of letters from Perry to the Chinese on Okinawa.

In 1856 Samuel Wells Williams became secretary and interpreter to the American Legation in China. He held this post until he left China in 1876. During this time he corresponded with Anson Burlingame, William Bradford Reed and William Henry Seward. The letters in this section make reference to the following events: 1858-revolution and riot in China and the struggle of foreign powers seeking greater freedom and toleration of missionaries; 1859-English, French and Chinese diplomatic conflict, new treaties with the U. S. and Claims; and 1862-rebel activity and destruction.
During the years 1871 to 1874 a great deal of the correspondence relates to Samuel Wells Williams's dictionary and the inadequacy of the American Legation in China.

The last eight years of Samuel Wells Williams's life were spent in the U. S. where he became the first professor of Chinese languages at Yale and president of the American Bible Society. He also revised his book, The Middle Kingdom . In this final stage there are many letters from missionaries remaining in China, the American Bible Society and voluminous correspondence from Scribners concerning the publishing of The Middle Kingdom .

The remaining correspondence (four boxes) covers the period 1885 to 1939. Personal Correspondence composes 90% or more of this section of the collection. The period from 1885 to 1890 contains more correspondence from Scribners and letters praising Frederick Wells Williams's book about his father. From 1903 the correspondence consists of letters from Wayland Wells Williams's mother, sister and friends. Frederick Wells Williams, the son of Samuel Wells Williams, was a professor at Yale and his son Wayland Wells Williams was a writer.

The collection also includes an annotated copy of the 1848 edition of Williams's The Middle Kingdom , along with a variety of letters, bills, clippings, and illustrations that were interleaved in the two volumes. There are also two journals, which appear to have been written by Williams after the events discussed. One chronicles Williams's trip to Japan with Commodore Matthew Perry, while the other records a trip to Peking in 1858-1859 and seems to be a compilation of extracts from his letters.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Archival Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Williams Family Letters (GEN MSS 761). General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Biographical Sketch
bioghist.p: Samuel Wells Williams (1812-1884): missionary to China, 1833-1845; printer and author; helped prepare Chinese dictionary; accompanied Perry expedition to Japan as interpreter in 1853-1854; in 1856 became secretary and interpreter of the American Legation to China, and remained until 1876; in 1877 became professor of Chinese at Yale University.

otherfindaid.head: Index
otherfindaid.p: For a partial listing of correspondents with Samuel Wells Williams, please consult theCorrespondents Index.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown, though much of the material in this collection is likely in the public domain. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

Williams, Samuel Wells, 1812-1884; 1 autograph letter signed to Mr. and Mrs. Robert Stanton Williams; 1881 January 26, New Haven

Blodget, Henry, 1825-1903; 1 autograph letter signed to Samuel Wells Williams; 1871 November 8, Peking, China; on verso: 1 autograph letter signed of Samuel Wells Williams to Mrs. Samuel Wells Williams; no date, no place

Williams, Samuel Wells, 1812-1884; autograph letters signed to William Frederick Williams; 1841 January 2; 1841 March 29; 1841 May 15; 1841 May 31; 1841 June 15; 1841 July 1; 1841 July 28; 1841 September 29; 1841 December 10; 1842 May 25; 1842 June 25; 1842 August 15; 1842 September 2; 1843 January 20; 1843 February 12; 1843 February 28; 1843 March 20; 1843 May 29; 1843 June 15; 1843 September 27; 1843 November 20; 1843 December 18; 1844 January 24; 1844 March 19; 1844 March 31; 1844 May 1; 1845 March 10; 1845 December 22; 1849 February 28; 1849 March 26; 1849 October 28; 1851 February 25; 1853 September 24; 1853 December 6; 1855 March 12; 1855 August 8; 1855 October 12; 1855 December 13; 1856 February 13; 1856 May 8; 1856 July 22; 1856 October 7; 1857 January 27; 1857 March 13; 1857 April 25; 1857 June 22; 1857 July 23; 1857 August 20; 1857 November 11; 1858 January 26; 1858 March 19; 1858 April 30; 1858 May 25; 1858 July 9; 1858 September 22; 1858 October 22; 1858 December 7; 1859 January 11; 1859 March 12; 1859 April 24; 1859 June 13; 1859 July 5; 1859 July 29: 1859 September 2; 1859 October 11; 1859 November 5; 1859 December 28; 1860 March 6; 1860 September 7; 1860 December 22; 1861 November 15; 1862 January 13; 1862 April 5; 1862 April 18: 1862 April 25; 1862 November 16; 1863 February 12; 1863 February 27; 1863 September 12; 1863 November 26; 1864 June 1; 1864 August 12; 1864 December 1; 1864 December 5; 1865 August 29; 1865 October 25; 1866 May 26; 1866 June 28; 1866 August 17; 1867 January 12; 1867 November 7

Williams, Samuel Wells, 1812-1884; 24 autograph letters signed to Mr. and Mrs. William Frederick Williams; various places; 1849 July 24; 1849 September 12; 1850 March 29; 1850 May 22; 1850 July 20; 1850 November 27; 1851 January 28; 1851 April 23; 1851 September 21; 1851 December 25; 1852 April 22; 1852 June 21; 1852 July 21; 1852 October 28; 1852 December 20; 1853 January 26; 1853 April 16; 1853 July 16; 1853 August 20; 1854 January 30; 1854 March 20; 1854 March 25; 1854 July 13; 1854 October 9
Williams, Samuel Wells, 1812-1884; 1 autograph letter signed to teachers and scholars of the First Presbyterian Church of Utica; 1866 May 31, Peking, China
Williams, Samuel Wells, 1812-1884; 1 autograph letter signed to an unidentified person; 1865 December, no day, Peking, China
Holcombe, Chester, 1844-1912; 1 autograph letter signed to Samuel Wells Williams; 1879 July 5, Peking, China

Holcombe, Chester, 1844-1912; 1 autograph letter signed to Samuel Wells Williams; also enclosed is a copy of that lease signed by Samuel Wells Williams, 1879 September 20, New Haven, and the William Maxwell Evarts, 1879 October 13, no place

Seward, George Frederick, 1840-1910; 1 autograph letter signed to Samuel Wells Williams; 1866 January 29, Shanghai; on verso: Williams' acceptance to join the North China branch of the Royal Asiatic Society of which Seward was president, 1866 April 3, Shanghai

Williams, Mrs. Sarah Walworth, 1815-1881; 17 autograph letters signed to Frederick Wells Williams; various places; 1873 March 23; 1873 June 26; 1873 July 12; 1873 July 16; 1873 August 16; 1873 August 26; 1879 September 5; 1880 March 2; 1880 March 12; 1880 March 19; 1880 April 18; 1880 August 9; 1880 August 22; 1880 September 16; 1880 September 29; 1880 October 6; 1880 October 15
Williams, Mrs. Sarah (Walworth), 1815-1881; 5 autograph letters signed to Mrs. William Williams; 1855 January 13; 1855 February 13; 1855 May 6; 1855 August no day; 1855 September 21
Williams, Mrs. Sarah Walworth, 1815-1881; 1 autograph letter signed to Mrs. William Frederick Williams; 1851 December 25, no place
Williams, Mrs. Sarah Walworth, 1815-1881; 1 autograph letter signed to William Frederick Williams; 1854 April 18, Canton, China
Williams, Talcott, 1849-1928; 1 autograph letter signed to Frederick Wells Williams; 1912 no month, no day, Philadelphia
Woolsey, Theodore Dwight, 1801-1889; 1 autograph letter signed to Samuel Wells Williams; 1877 October 24, no place

Yates, Matthew Tyson, 1819-1888; 10 autograph letters signed to Samuel Wells Williams; 1877 June 29, Shanghai; 1878 May no day; 1879 February 3; 1879 May 26; 1879 July 29; 1879 September 30; 1879 November 29; 1879 December 20; 1880 April 29; 1880 August 9

Yung, Wing, 1828-1912; 8 autograph letters signed to Samuel Wells Williams; 1849 April 15, various places; 1850 December 25; 1852 December 30; 1853 July 27; 1877 February 19; 1877 February 21; 1877 June 7; 1878 August 23

persname.part: Jackson, A. V. Williams (Abraham Valentine Williams), 1862-1937
Martin, W. A. P. (William Alexander Parsons), 1827-1916

corpname.part: United States Naval Expedition to Japan (1852-1854)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A letter from this folder has been separated out for preservation into box 2A, folder 47A.

did.unittitle: Japanese petition attributed to the hand of Yoshida Shoin
Kuwanouhi and Isagi brief note attributed to Yoshida Shoin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note: It has not been possible to distinguish the papers of S.W. Williams from those of his son, F.W. Williams, with perfect certainty. In particular, some items filed under SWW may pertain to FWW.

did.unittitle: A Syllabic Dictionary of the Chinese Language …
List of missionaries who received copies at $6.00
Loose material found in Volumes I and II
Part of printed copy, with manuscript corrections
"List of Articles by S. Wells Williams in the Chinese Repository "
List of letters to W.F. Williams
"Description of the town of Hakodadi, in Japan": reprint

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Letter to President Hayes by the Faculty of Yale College, on Chinese immigration: reprint, with annotations

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: "Notes of remarks to Linonians," on Chinese immigration: manuscript
Draft of letter to the editor of the New York Tribune on Chinese immigration to and rights in America

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Note and extracts from letters on the application of treaty provisions to Americans residing in China and Japan

did.unittitle: Article on the Lewchew Islands: manuscript
Draft and translations on the place of women in Chinese culture

did.unittitle: Draft of general article on the Chinese language

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Draft of article on learning the Chinese language
Article on the development of Chinese writing: manuscript
Draft of North American Review article on historical value of early Chinese records
"A Chinese Historical Novel Lieh Kwoh Chi, or The Records of the Feudal Kingdoms : with a translation of Chapters I and II": reprint, with manuscript translation of chapters I-IX
Translation of Lieh Kwoh Chi chapters IX-XIX: manuscript
Princeton lectures on Chinese religion and philosophy: manuscript
"Nature of the Chinese religious and political institutions and reasons for their perpetuity": manuscript
Review of two books on Christian merchants' missionary work in China: manuscript
"The Controversy among the Protestant Missionaries on the Proper Translation of the Words God and Spirit into Chinese, Bibliotheca Sacra " : manuscript

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: " Shin : Does it mean God or Spirit ?": manuscript

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous lists of, and notes on, Chinese orthography

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: "Lists of plants in the treatise on grains and planting," in Chinese and English
"System of Orthography commonly used in writing the Amoy Dialect,"
Gazetteer of Chinese districts and cities
Chronology and survey of Chinese history; lists of Chinese emperors and reigns, and Japanese diaries; Chinese historical survey by states
Expense account of his trip from China through the Near East and Europe to the United States and of his sojourn in the United States; calling card and papers laid in
Account book, mostly of personal accounts, kept in Shanghai, Macao, and Hong Kong, during mission work, and at Legation in Peking; calling card and note laid in
Account book and diary of his trip from China through the Near East and Europe to the United States
The National Diary, 1881 , with manuscript entries, including a few by Sarah (Walworth) Williams
Statements of account for Williams's books
Accounts, correspondence, and circulars re: payments for Williams's work for the Japan Expedition and for the Legation at Peking
Register of receipts from an auction of Williams's belongings, with buyers' names written in
Miscellaneous accounts, receipts, and bills of sale
Journal: trip to Japan with Commodore Perry

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The journal is digitized. Researchers must use the digital use copies instead of the originals.
This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 22 instead of the originals.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The material is digitized. Researchers must use the digital use copies instead of the originals.
This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 22 instead of the originals.

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Claim against the Chinese Government for losses suffered, and proposal re: surplus paid to U.S. authorities
Agreement between the British and Foreign Bible Society and the American Bible Society re: editions of translations of the Bible
Power of attorney, and memorandum concerning lease of Legation at Peking
Copies of petition to Congress for reimbursement of salary, with reply
Writings about Williams, some by family members, and a resolution adopted by the Peking Missionary Association at his death
Williams family genealogies, one with a preface by Williams
Papers regarding ordination, minutes of a meeting of The Congregational Association of China, certificates and licenses, expense account, account of Robert Morrison's estate, and obituary clipping
Manuscript copy of an English translation of Lao Tse, The Book of the Way and of Virtue; prepared for Bridgman by a student

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Thomas George Grosvenor: "Yun-Nan Inquiry": private accounts while at British Legation at Peking (in same volume with following item)
Sophia Gardiner (Williams) Grosvenor Gray: household accounts while at The Hague
Ko Kun Hua: Chinese and English poetry dedicated to Williams; correspondence, calling cards, and clippings laid in
Susan Schereschewsky: birthday poem to Williams, with his reply
Tung Ta-jin: "Stanzas on the departure of Anson Burlingame"
U.S. Legation at Peking: Centennial observance: printed poem and programs, and manuscript poems by Chauncey Goodrich, George Frederick Seward, and [Chester Holcombe?]; also another patriotic poem
T.F. Wade and Tung Siun: Chinese translation of Longfellow's "Psalm of Life"
Unidentified: "A few lines on the occasion of Dr. Williams's leaving China"
Biographical notices and obituaries of Williams, and reviews of Life and Letters of SWW
Scrapbook of obituary clippings on Williams
Reviews of A Syllabic Dictionary of the Chinese Language …

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Members of the North China Mission
Scrapbook of calling cards, correspondence, and printed memorabilia

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The scrapbook is digitized. Researchers must use the digital use copies instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Chinese documents, some with Williams's annotations
Chinese documents, some with Williams's translations or annotations, and one with silk envelope
Miscellaneous Chinese documents and printed matter, including a document with postage stamps enclosed
Chinese books, including translations of English works
Chinese imperial examination essay written by Lu Runxiang in Emperor Tongzhi's court
Lithographs by W. Heine for Matthew C. Perry's Narrative of the Expedition of an American Squadron to the China Seas and Japan
Manuscript maps of China, in Chinese
Printed maps of China, in English

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note: It has not been possible to distinguish the papers of S.W. Williams from those of his son, F.W. Williams, with perfect certainty. In particular, some items filed under SWW may pertain to FWW.

did.unittitle: "Tito Melenia as a Study of Character"
"Scholasticism and Confucianism--the Civilization of the Middle Ages and of the Chinese"
Hermann Grimm, "The History of German Art"
Anson Burlingame and the First Chinese Mission to Foreign Powers : notes, draft of preface, printed matter, agreement with publishers
"A Pioneer of German Art: Asmus Jakob Carstens": reprint
"The Historical Development of the Relations between the United States and China": typescript
"Chinese Emigrants in the Far East," American Historical Association: manuscript, program
"The Inauguration of Representative Government in China": typescript
"Yuan and China's Struggle for Freedom": typescript
China: history and politics: manuscripts, printed matter
Asian architecture, and Chinese and Japanese literature: manuscripts
China: history and culture: manuscript, typescript
China: religion (originally in envelope labelled "(Cambridge) 1912," apparently sent (back?) to Williams by Prof. Irving Fisher): manuscripts
"History of the Ancient World" (originally "Ancient Oriental Nations from the Earliest Times")
Syllabi and bibliographies, some by other professors
Papers of students: ancient history, colonization, the Philippines, and the Hawaiian Islands

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a petition for the elimination of Williams's June examination.

did.unittitle: Diary kept in New Haven, New York, and Utica, with calling cards laid in
Statements of account from stay in Germany
Statements of account, bills, receipts, stock-holders' report, including tax receipts for Martha Wayland
Copies of publishers' agreement with H.L. Wayland re: Francis Wayland as an Educator
Photographs: Macao Protestant Cemetery; interior of study
Alfred Thayer Mahan, "The War on the Sea and its Lessons"
The Holiness Baptist , later The Evangel : incomplete run of issues, with 2 reports
Receipt from Japanese priest, on the occasion of the presentation of two Ryokai Mandala scrolls to Williams (possibly those in folio 27)
Pieces of tree bark, with lettering on them
"Translations of the Bible into Chinese": manuscript
Expense account kept in China, Europe, and America
Printed diary with manuscript entries and accounts, kept chiefly in New Haven
Editions of Letts's Pocket Diary, and an Almanac , with manuscript entries and accounts, kept in China, Europe, and America

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Scraps-China" contains primarily newspaper clippings relating to Chinese and Japanese culture and history.

did.unittitle: Williams, Cornelius Wade, miscellaneous volume of notes, letters, mounted clippings, accounts, and scribblings; kept at Milford, Conn.
Memoranda, minutes, treasurer's reports, and budgets
Statement of Acheen's claim for indemnity for losses
Bill of sale for a girl, Macao
Catalogue, in Chinese, of books read by SWW
Official letter from the military governor Li at Fu P'ing (Kuangsi) to a group of foreigners
Letter from the governor of a High Commissioner sent by the king asking for a reply to his previous letter
Photographs of the interior of the study of Frederick Wells Williams
Passports, appointments, and college diploma of Samuel Wells Williams and Frederick Wells Williams
Loose material found in journal of trip to Japan with Commodore Perry
Manuscript maps mounted on Chinese scrolls, captioned as follows by SWW or FWW
"West Manchuria, Liantung, Chihli, Shantung south to Fuhkien"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence is primarily from Robert Stanton Williams of Utica, New York, to his brother Samuel Wells Williams regarding family matters and finances. There are several 1853-1854 typed transcriptions of letters from William Frederic Williams to Rev. Leonard Bacon, of New Haven, written from Mosul. Also included are a letter from Matthew C. Perry to Mr. Lao Shumming regarding his employment as a translator, and a letter from Perry addressed to Samuel Wells Williams in Canton.

did.unittitle: Printed pamphlets and articles by and about Samuel Wells Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gilman, Rev. E. W., American Heroes on Mission Field, No. 8, S. Wells Williams, LL.D. ; Williams, Samuel Wells: Notices of Fu-Sang, and Other Countries Laying East of China (1880), Education of Woman in China , The First and Second Reports of the Medical Missionary Society in China (1841), and Bulletin of the American Geographical Society (1884, No. 2).

did.unittitle: Williams, Huntington, Huntington Williams, M.D. , published for family and friends, Baltimore
"Answer Respecting Limits," Japanese (written in Chinese characters) text on onion skin paper (1 sheet), accompanied by photostatic copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Document relating to the treaty with the United States that opened Japan to foreign trade, and described by Samuel Wells Williams in his "Journal of the Perry Expedition to Japan," Transactions of the Asiatic Society of Japan (1910), pages 191-192.

did.unittitle: Williams, Robert S. (brother of Samuel Wells Williams)
Genealogical material relating to Thomas Williams (grandfather of Samuel Wells Williams)
